source.title: Emergency Disconnect Sequence Event - Transocean Deepwater Poseidon

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 391

event.top_event: Emergency Disconnect Sequence due to vessel excursion
actions_taken: ROV inspection of well, BOP, and riser performed
Drill pipe and MPD bearing recovered from drill shack
Damaged components sent ashore for inspection
NRC reports submitted for synthetic based mud release
event.summary: On March 5, 2024, at 04:15 hrs, the Transocean Deepwater Poseidon drillship experienced extreme unexpected changes in wind speed and direction due to strong thunderstorms while drilling the AW003 well in MC 391. Wind speeds escalated from 30 kts to 83 kts with rapid directional shifts that were not forecasted. The vessel excursion triggered an Emergency Disconnect Sequence when the vessel approached the red watch circle limit (90.3 ft). During the EDS, three joints of 6-5/8 in drill pipe and an MPD bearing assembly (13,200 lbs) fell from the derrick and struck the drill shack protective barrier. Approximately 2,875 bbls of synthetic based mud (1,667 bbls base oil) was released to the seafloor. Total estimated damages: $1,000,000.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time
key_phrases: extreme unexpected changes in wind speed and direction
drill pipe and MPD bearing fell
weather forecasts did not predict adverse conditions
vessel radar did not show actual weather conditions

hazards.name: Adverse weather conditions (thunderstorms with extreme wind)
Drill pipe and equipment falling from derrick
Loss of containment of drilling fluids

threats.name: Unforecasted rapid wind speed increase and directional shift
Failure to maintain well control during vessel movement
Inadequate securing of drill pipe in derrick during dynamic event
Riser and BOP damage during emergency disconnect

consequences.name: Structural damage to LMRP, BOP, and riser components
Drill pipe and MPD bearing impact damage to drill shack protective barrier
Release of 2,875 bbls synthetic based mud to seafloor
Potential personnel injury from falling equipment

controls.name: Weather forecasting and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: early warning system for adverse weather
controls.lod_basis: primary prevention control to avoid hazardous conditions

supporting_text: The weather forecasts did not predict the adverse weather conditions that were witnessed on location
At 04:03 hrs, the wind shifted to the West and intensified to 60 kts, which was not forecasted
As the vessel attempted to adjust its heading responding to the wind, the wind speed dropped to zero momentarily before shifting again to the North and escalating to 83 kts

controls.name: Vessel radar weather detection
controls.lod_basis: primary detection system for localized weather hazards

supporting_text: The vessel's radar did not show the actual weather conditions that were witnessed on location

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning system with watch circles
controls.barrier_role: maintain vessel position within operational limits
controls.lod_basis: secondary control to prevent vessel excursion beyond safe limits

supporting_text: At 04:00 hrs., the excursion initially reached the Yellow Watch Circle (as defined by the Well Specific Operational Criteria Report) that immediately raised the Dynamic Positioning (DP) Yellow Alert
As the drillship approached the red watch circle limit (90.3 ft), the Driller was on speakerphone, being informed of distances off well center

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect Sequence procedure
controls.barrier_role: disconnect well and prevent loss of well control during vessel excursion
controls.lod_basis: critical mitigation control to prevent blowout and loss of containment

supporting_text: The decision to initiate the EDS was made by the Shell Company Representative and the EDS sequence was functioned from the Driller's console
The driller-initiated EDS Mode 2 button was pressed from Drill Floor, which completed a 68-sec sequence where the casing shear was fired first, followed by the upper blind shear ram second
confirmation of a successful EDS was received

controls.name: Drill pipe hang-off procedure in middle pipe ram
controls.barrier_role: secure drill string during vessel movement to prevent well control loss
controls.lod_basis: procedural control to maintain well integrity during dynamic positioning event

supporting_text: the Driller on tour had already begun to place the 6-5/8 in drill pipe in a hang-off position once the top drive was made up
The drill string was lowered to a space-out position and the middle pipe ram was closed
Once the gallon count was confirmed, the Driller continued to hang off with a 500k lb. hook load

controls.name: Derrick equipment securing and lashing
controls.barrier_role: prevent falling objects from derrick during vessel motion
controls.lod_basis: primary control to prevent dropped objects hazard

supporting_text: During the EDS event, three joints of 6-5/8 in pipe and a Managed Pressure Drilling (MPD) bearing (that were all connected together in one stand) fell from its position in the derrick on the auxiliary side of the drill floor and landed on top of the Driller's shack
This pipe and bearing assembly weighed approximately 13,200 lbs

controls.name: Protective barrier around drill shack
controls.barrier_role: protect personnel and equipment from falling objects
controls.lod_basis: tertiary control to mitigate consequences of dropped objects

supporting_text: The metal protective barrier on top and on the side of the Driller's shack was damaged due to this impact

controls.name: Riser and BOP design for emergency disconnect
controls.barrier_role: safely disconnect riser and prevent fluid loss during EDS
controls.lod_basis: critical mitigation control to minimize environmental release during emergency

supporting_text: There was approximately 2875 bbls of Synthetic Based Mud (Bara ECD), of which contained 1,667 bbls of base oil that was released to the seafloor
LMRP Mandrel/Connector, Rubber goods, BOP - General parts replacement and sheared control fluid, misc. parts (ring gaskets, drill pipe drag across LMRP connector upon EDS)

controls.name: ROV inspection and well verification
controls.barrier_role: verify well integrity and identify damage after EDS
controls.lod_basis: post-event verification to confirm well security and assess damage

supporting_text: the Remote Operated Vehicle (ROV) prepared to dive for an inspection of the well, Blowout Preventers (BOPs), and Riser
The ROV did verify that the well was secure

controls.name: Incident reporting and investigation procedures
controls.barrier_role: ensure complete and timely reporting of all incident aspects
controls.lod_basis: post-event control to ensure regulatory compliance and learning

supporting_text: This event with the pipe and bearing assembly falling across the drill floor was never brought up or mentioned during the onsite interviews with Transocean personnel
Transocean's investigation report did not include this event, nor did Shell reveal this information during interviews and email notifications

This aspect of the incident was discovered by BSEE investigators while conducting an inspection on June 24, 2024, when inspectors noticed the drill shack's protective barrier was damaged and inquired how it happened
The District Manager was not notified with a written statement within 15 days of the incident that resulted in damages greater than $25,000

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
